by_judge.judge: C M POONACHA WILL SIT IN COURT HALL NO.6

narrative:
On June 9, 2026, the Karnataka High Court registered an overall hearing rate of 47.5%, addressing 1,275 cases out of 2,687 scheduled across 44 judges. This workload clearance represents an efficiency increase from the 41.4% hearing rate (1,034 of 2,497) recorded on June 5 and the 46.2% rate (1,189 of 2,572) on June 4. However, today’s activity remains below the peak performance observed on June 1, which achieved a 52.3% hearing rate. 

Individual judicial output varied significantly across court halls. Justice V. Srishananda in Court Hall 21 managed a heavy docket, successfully hearing 95 of 141 scheduled cases (67.4%). Justice M. Nagaprasanna in Court Hall 14 demonstrated high efficiency, resolving 72 of 104 cases (69.2%) on his primary list and 54 of 75 cases (72.0%) on his secondary list. Justice H.P. Sandesh in Court Hall 8 also achieved a high conversion rate, hearing 56 of 66 cases (84.8%). Conversely, Court Hall 19 under Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty was assigned an exceptional schedule of 342 cases, ultimately concluding hearings for 72 of them (21.1%).

Procedural stage analysis indicates that Orders comprised the largest volume with 934 scheduled matters, achieving a 47.6% hearing rate. Admissions converted at 50.8% (232 of 457), while Final Hearings continued to lag with a 29.6% rate (138 of 466). In terms of case-type distribution, Writ Petitions remained the most prominent category with 1,243 scheduled and 503 heard (40.5%), a clearance rate highly consistent with June 5 (42.7%) and June 4 (42.6%). Miscellaneous First Appeals maintained a steady 48.9% hearing rate, while Regular Second Appeals yielded a strong 67.9% conversion rate.
